scores.correctness_notes: Core concepts match gold standard: fail() doesn't stop execution, exception crashes and may put unit in error state, fail() is the recommended graceful approach. Adds accurate bonus detail about set_results() still being callable after fail().
scores.hallucination_notes: Minor: claims 'Juju framework catches the exception' in action context — gold standard says unhandled exceptions cause charm to 'crash and enter error state' requiring 'juju resolve'. The agent's framing slightly softens this. Also claims exception gives 'traceback' as action failure message, which may not be precisely accurate per gold standard (which says error details are only in Juju log, not action output). These are minor inaccuracies rather than invented APIs.
